tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

reply_to_id.description: Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":58540,"title":"[AgentServer Responses] Docs: Document persistence failure resilience and request-correlation headers","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` **Triggered by:** PR #58526 (commit ab77838) by `@RaviPidaparthi` ## What Changed PR #58526 released `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` 1.0.0-beta.4 with these new features in `BinaryDataExpansionHelpers.cs` and the server middleware: - **Persistence failure resilience** — storage failures no longer crash or stall responses; they now complete gracefully with `status: "failed"` and `error.code: "storage_error"`. Covers streaming, background+streaming, background+non-streaming, and synchronous modes. - **`x-request-id` response header** — all endpoints now return this header for request correlation; its value is also injected into `error.additionalInfo.request_id` on error responses. - **`traceparent` header in Foundry storage logging** — W3C distributed trace ID is now included in every log entry from `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`, enabling correlation with backend traces. - **`GetInputExpanded` content normalization** — string shorthand on `ItemMessage.Content` is now auto-expanded to the canonical array form. ## Gaps Found - The **Troubleshooting** section in `README.md` does not document the `storage_error` error code or explain what `status: "failed"` means when a storage operation fails, leaving operators without guidance on how to detect and respond to these failures. - The **Logging** section in `README.md` mentions OpenTelemetry activity source but does not mention the `traceparent` header propagation in Foundry storage logging or how to correlate SDK log entries with backend distributed traces. - There is **no documentation** anywhere in the README or samples about the `x-request-id` response header or how callers can use `error.additionalInfo.request_id` for client-side error correlation. - The **input normalization behavior** (string shorthand → canonical array) changed in beta.4; this implicit contract is not documented in the README's `ResponseContext` / `GetInputItemsAsync` sections, which could confuse implementors relying on the raw `Content` shape. - There is **no sample** demonstrating error handling for storage failures or how to extract correlation IDs from error responses. 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — edit **1a. Update the Troubleshooting → Common errors section** Locate the "Common errors" subsection and append the following bullet after the existing entries: ```markdown - **Storage failure (`error.code: "storage_error"`)**: A backend storage operation failed after the response began executing. The response is returned with `status: "failed"` and `error.code: "storage_error"` rather than leaving it permanently stuck at `in_progress`. For streaming responses, the terminal `response.failed` SSE event is emitted automatically. For synchronous responses, an HTTP 500 is returned with the storage error details. Check the `x-request-id` response header (or `error.additionalInfo.request_id` in the error body) to correlate the failure with backend logs. ``` **1b. Expand the Logging subsection** Locate the Logging subsection (currently: "The library emits OpenTelemetry traces via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source...") and expand it to: ```markdown ### Logging The library emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source. Enable logging in your ASP.NET Core application to diagnose issues. #### Request correlation Every response from the server includes an `x-request-id` response header. The value is resolved from the OTEL trace ID → incoming `x-request-id` header → a generated GUID. On error responses, the same value is injected into `error.additionalInfo.request_id` in the response body, enabling client-side correlation without needing access to server logs. #### Foundry storage logging `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` logs every outbound Foundry storage API call at `Information` level, including the HTTP method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-ms-client-request-id`, `x-request-id`, `apim-request-id`). Each log entry also includes the `traceparent` header (W3C Trace Context format), enabling direct correlation between SDK log entries and backend distributed traces in any W3C-compatible tracing system (Application Insights, Jaeger, Zipkin, etc.). ``` **1c. Update ResponseContext documentation (Key Concepts section)** In the `ResponseContext` bullet list (after the `RawBody` entry), add a note about `GetInputItemsAsync` normalizing string shorthand: ```markdown - **Content normalization**: When `input` contains `ItemMessage` entries whose `content` is a plain JSON string (e.g., `"Hello"`), the library automatically expands it to the canonical array form (`[{"type":"input_text","text":"Hello"}]`). `GetInputItemsAsync` and `GetInputTextAsync` always return normalized content regardless of how the client submitted the request. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new snippet test files are required for the prose-only additions above. However, if a sample for error handling is desired: 1. **Write the sample code** at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Samples/SampleErrorHandling.cs` - Demonstrate reading `x-request-id` from an error response and logging `error.code` - Wrap the relevant code with `#region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ErrorCorrelation` / `#endregion` 2. **Add placeholder in README** in the Troubleshooting section: ``` ``` ``` ``` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` already contains all required sections: 1. ✅ Getting started (Install, Prerequisites, Configure the server) 2. ✅ Key concepts 3. ✅ Examples 4. ⚠️ **Troubleshooting** — missing `storage_error` and correlation header documentation (fix in Step 1a and 1b above) 5. ✅ Next steps 6. ✅ Contributing ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — injects snippet content into README placeholders; check `git diff` to confirm README updated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no public API changes expected for doc-only edits; run to verify 5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > [!WARNING] > > ⚠️ Firewall blocked 1 domain > > The following domain was blocked by the firewall during workflow execution: > > - `centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com` > > To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter: > > ```yaml > network: > allowed: > - defaults > - "centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com" > ``` > > See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information. > > > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24813836150/agentic_workflow) · ● 598.6K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58540","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-23T02:49:57Z","updated_at":"2026-04-23T02:49:57Z"}

The value is resolved from the OTEL trace ID → incoming `x-request-id` header → a generated GUID. The equivalent manual setup (`AddInvocationsServer()` + `MapInvocationsServer()`) is missing. - `InvocationsServerOptions` is part of the public API but is never mentioned or described in the README.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations/README.md` — Edit #### 1a. Expand the `InvocationContext` key concept subsection Replace the existing `### InvocationContext` subsection (currently two sentences): ```markdown ### InvocationContext Provides request metadata to the handler, including the resolved session ID and forwarded client headers. ``` with: ```markdown ### InvocationContext Provides request metadata to the handler. All properties are read-only and resolved before `HandleAsync` is called. | Property | Type | Description | |----------|------|-------------| | `InvocationId` | `string` | Unique identifier for this invocation. Passed as the first argument to `GetAsync` and `CancelAsync`. Use as a key when storing per-invocation state. | | `SessionId` | `string` | Resolved multi-turn session identifier (extracted from query parameters or `INVOCATIONS_SESSION_ID` environment variable). | | `ClientHeaders` | `IReadOnlyDictionary` | Forwarded `x-client-*` headers from the original request — useful for propagating tracing context and client metadata. | | `QueryParameters` | `IReadOnlyDictionary` | All query parameters from the incoming request. | | `Isolation` | `IsolationContext` | Isolation context extracted from `x-agent-user-isolation-key` and `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` headers. Useful for multi-tenant scenarios where per-user or per-chat data must be isolated. `IsolationContext.Empty` indicates no isolation headers were present. | ``` #### 1b. Add a manual DI setup example in `### Configure the server` After the existing Tier 1 snippet block and `EchoHandler` code block (after the closing paragraph about `AgentHostBuilder`), add a new subsection: ```markdown For full control over the ASP.NET Core host, register services manually in your `Program.cs`: ```C# Snippet:Invocations_ReadMe_ManualSetup var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(); builder.Services.AddInvocationsServer(); builder.Services.AddScoped(); var app = builder.Build(); app.MapInvocationsServer(); app.Run(); ``` ``` #### 1c. Document `InvocationsServerOptions` in the `### Customizing the host` subsection Append after the existing tier sample links: ```markdown To configure `InvocationsServerOptions` (for future options), pass a configure delegate: ```csharp InvocationsServer.Run(configure: builder => { builder.AddInvocations(options => { // InvocationsServerOptions configuration here }); }); ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets #### Snippet: `Invocations_ReadMe_ManualSetup` **Test file:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations/tests/Snippets/ReadMeSnippets.cs` Add a new test method to the existing `ReadMeSnippets.cs` file: ```csharp [Test] public async Task ManualSetupSnippet() { #region Snippet:Invocations_ReadMe_ManualSetup var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(); builder.Services.AddInvocationsServer(); builder.Services.AddScoped(); var app = builder.Build(); app.MapInvocationsServer(); app.Run(); #endregion #if !SNIPPET // Test-only: just verify compilation; don't actually run await Task.CompletedTask; #endif } ``` **README placeholder location:** In the `### Configure the server` subsection of `README.md`, add immediately after the `EchoHandler` class snippet: ````markdown ```C# Snippet:Invocations_ReadMe_ManualSetup ``` ```` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The existing README already contains all required sections in the correct order: 1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Configure the server) 2. ✅ Key concepts 3. ✅ Examples 4. ✅ Troubleshooting 5. ✅ Next steps 6. ✅ Contributing Missing/incomplete subsections to update: - `### InvocationContext` — update per Step 1a above - `### Configure the server` — add manual setup per Step 1b above Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirms README is updated with the new snippet 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — only needed if any public types changed (no public API changes expected here) 5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations/`

Responses] Docs: document EmitAnnotationAdded builder lifecycle and RefusalContentBuilder full API","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` **Triggered by:** [REDACTED] (PR #58094) by `@RaviPidaparthi` ## What Changed PR #58094 refactored the builder lifecycle so that each content builder owns its full event lifecycle end-to-end: - `TextContentBuilder`: introduced `EmitTextDone(string?)` for `response.output_text.done` and a separate `EmitDone()` for `response.content_part.done`; added `EmitAnnotationAdded(Annotation)` (0+) between the two; added an `Annotations` property so `OutputItemMessageBuilder.EmitDone()` auto-builds the final message from tracked children - `RefusalContentBuilder`: introduced `EmitRefusalDone(string)` for `response.refusal.done` and a separate `EmitDone()` for `response.content_part.done` - `OutputItemMessageBuilder`: `AddTextContent`/`AddRefusalContent` now auto-track child builders; `EmitDone()` builds from them (removed manual `EmitContentDone()` overloads) - `OutputItemReasoningItemBuilder`: `AddSummaryPart` auto-tracks children; removed `EmitSummaryPartDone()` ## Gaps Found - **`EmitAnnotationAdded` is undocumented in all markdown docs.** The full `TextContentBuilder` lifecycle is `EmitAdded → EmitDelta (0+) → EmitTextDone → EmitAnnotationAdded (0+) → EmitDone`, but no documentation or sample shows the `EmitAnnotationAdded` step. A developer who needs to stream text deltas _and_ attach annotations has no reference for how to use the full builder API. - **`Sample15_Annotations.md` only demonstrates the convenience generator** (`OutputItemMessage(text, annotations)`). There is no sample showing the full builder API flow that combines streaming deltas with `EmitAnnotationAdded`. The `response.output_text.annotation.added` SSE event produced by `EmitAnnotationAdded` is never mentioned in any markdown doc. - **`RefusalContentBuilder` builder API is undocumented.** `handler-implementation-guide.md` names "text + refusal" as the primary reason to use the builder API but provides no code example for `AddRefusalContent()` → `EmitRefusalDone()` → `EmitDone()`.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### 1a. `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/docs/handler-implementation-guide.md` **Edit** — in the "Text Messages → Using builders (fine-grained control)" subsection, extend the existing code block after `EmitTextDone` to show the annotation step. Replace the current example ending: ```csharp yield return text.EmitTextDone("First chunk of text. Second chunk. "); yield return text.EmitDone(); yield return message.EmitDone(); ``` with: ```csharp yield return text.EmitTextDone("First chunk of text. Second chunk. "); // Optionally attach annotations (file refs, citations, URLs) after finalizing text. // Each call produces a response.output_text.annotation.added SSE event. yield return text.EmitAnnotationAdded(new FilePath(fileId: "/reports/summary.pdf", index: 0)); yield return text.EmitDone(); // response.content_part.done — includes all annotations yield return message.EmitDone(); ``` Add a note: _`OutputItemMessageBuilder.EmitDone()` reads the `Annotations` property on each tracked `TextContentBuilder` to build the final `content[].annotations` array automatically — no manual wiring needed._ **Edit** — immediately after the text builder example, add a "Refusal content" example: ````csharp // Emitting refusal content with the full builder API var message = stream.AddOutputItemMessage(); yield return message.EmitAdded(); var refusal = message.AddRefusalContent(); yield return refusal.EmitAdded(); yield return refusal.EmitDelta("I cannot help with that request."); yield return refusal.EmitRefusalDone("I cannot help with that request."); yield return refusal.EmitDone(); // response.content_part.done yield return message.EmitDone(); ``` **Edit** — update the builder lifecycle reference table (near line 433) to include the full `TextContentBuilder` step sequence: ``` TextContentBuilder: EmitAdded() → EmitDelta(chunk) × N → EmitTextDone(text?) → EmitAnnotationAdded(annotation) × N → EmitDone() RefusalContentBuilder: EmitAdded() → EmitDelta(chunk) × N → EmitRefusalDone(text) → EmitDone() ```` #### 1b. `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/samples/Sample15_Annotations.md` **Edit** — add a new section after the existing convenience generator example: ```markdown ## Using the full builder API (streaming deltas with annotations) When you need to stream text tokens incrementally _and_ attach annotations, use `AddTextContent()` with `EmitAnnotationAdded()`: ```C# Snippet:Responses_Sample15_StreamingAnnotationsHandler public class StreamingAnnotationsHandler : ResponseHandler { public override async IAsyncEnumerable CreateAsync( CreateResponse request, ResponseContext context, [EnumeratorCancellation] CancellationToken cancellationToken) { var stream = new ResponseEventStream(context, request); yield return stream.EmitCreated(); yield return stream.EmitInProgress(); var message = stream.AddOutputItemMessage(); yield return message.EmitAdded(); var text = message.AddTextContent(); yield return text.EmitAdded(); yield return text.EmitDelta("Here is the "); yield return text.EmitDelta("monthly summary. "); // Finalize text (merges accumulated deltas automatically when no override is passed) yield return text.EmitTextDone(); // Each EmitAnnotationAdded produces a response.output_text.annotation.added event yield return text.EmitAnnotationAdded(new FilePath(fileId: "/reports/monthly-summary.pdf", index: 0)); yield return text.EmitDone(); // response.content_part.done — annotations included yield return message.EmitDone(); // response.output_item.done yield return stream.EmitCompleted(); await Task.CompletedTask; } } \``` ``` #### 1c. `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Snippets/Sample15Snippets.cs` **Edit** — add a new `[Test]` method and `#region Snippet:Responses_Sample15_StreamingAnnotationsHandler` block implementing `StreamingAnnotationsHandler` (see 1b). Wrap any test-only assertions in `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif`. Follow the pattern of the existing `FileAnnotationsHandler` region in the same file. ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets 1. **Write the sample code** in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Snippets/Sample15Snippets.cs`: - Class `StreamingAnnotationsHandler` in `#region Snippet:Responses_Sample15_StreamingAnnotationsHandler` / `#endregion` - The snippet name must be exactly `Responses_Sample15_StreamingAnnotationsHandler` 2. **Add the placeholder** in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/samples/Sample15_Annotations.md`: ` ```C# Snippet:Responses_Sample15_StreamingAnnotationsHandler ` followed by closing ` ``` ` 3. Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/tests/samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs` as the canonical snippet pattern example. ### Step 3: Verify README structure `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` already has all required sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). No structural changes needed — the README links to `handler-implementation-guide.md` for builder API details. ### Step 4: Validate Run in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirm `Sample15_Annotations.md` is updated with the new snippet content 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no public API changes expected; confirm no diff 5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`

## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue documents gaps in `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` README.md — specifically missing documentation for persistence failure resilience (`storage_error`), `x-request-id` correlation header, `traceparent` propagation in Foundry storage logging, and `GetInputItemsAsync` content normalization introduced in beta.4.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` 1.0.0-beta.4  
**Affected API:** `BinaryDataExpansionHelpers.cs`, `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`, server middleware  
**Scenarios:**
- Troubleshooting section missing `storage_error` error code documentation and `status: "failed"` explanation
- Logging section missing `traceparent` header propagation and distributed trace correlation guidance
- No documentation on `x-request-id` response header or `error.additionalInfo.request_id` for client-side correlation
- Input normalization behavior (string shorthand → canonical array) undocumented in `ResponseContext`/`GetInputItemsAsync` sections
- No sample demonstrating error handling for storage failures or correlation ID extraction

**Root ask:** Update `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` with documentation for these new beta.4 features and add an error-handling sample.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only gap issue triggered by PR #58526. No runtime bug is reported.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review PR #58526 changes to `BinaryDataExpansionHelpers.cs` and server middleware to confirm the four feature areas described
2. Update `README.md` Troubleshooting section to document `storage_error` code, `status: "failed"` semantics, and `x-request-id` / `error.additionalInfo.request_id` correlation fields
3. Expand `README.md` Logging section to cover `traceparent` W3C header in `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` and how it enables backend trace correlation
4. Add a note in Key Concepts (`ResponseContext`) about `GetInputItemsAsync` content normalization from string shorthand to canonical array
5. Optionally add a `SampleErrorHandling.cs` sample demonstrating error correlation
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` is a data-plane client SDK under `/sdk/agentserver/`, not a management or provisioning package

- **Confidence:** High — the issue explicitly names the service directory and package; CODEOWNERS provides an unambiguous mapping; category is clearly Client (data-plane SDK)
